approach_rationale: This is a greenfield bootstrap of ~35 files. Conservative approach avoids gold-plating unspecified details (tileset art, full save UI) while delivering every required feature. Ideal approach would add real sprite sheets, a complete save/load screen, and full E2E coverage but would triple scope.

steps.description: Create branch af/201-project-foundation-playable-overworld-sl/1
Scaffold project config files: package.json, tsconfig.json, tailwind.config.ts, next.config.ts, vitest.config.ts, playwright.config.ts
Create app shell: app/layout.tsx (Press Start 2P font, dark bg), app/globals.css (Tailwind base + canvas reset), app/page.tsx (mounts GameCanvas)
Define all TypeScript type files: GameState.ts, PokemonTypes.ts, BattleTypes.ts, MapTypes.ts — pure interfaces/enums, no logic
Create data files: types.ts (TYPE_CHART), pokemon.ts (#1-20 + #25), moves.ts (~40 moves), items.ts (11 items)
Create map JSON files: pallet-town.json (20×20) and route-1.json (20×30) with terrain/objects/collision layers and encounter configs
Create entity classes: Player.ts, PokemonInstance.ts (with Gen 1 stat formula)
Create engine infrastructure: AssetLoader.ts (with fallback rects), Camera.ts (viewport clamp), InputManager.ts (keyboard + touch hooks), SaveSystem.ts (localStorage)
Create GameEngine.ts: fixed 16.67ms RAF loop, accumulator pattern, phase dispatch
Create OverworldSystem.ts: tile movement, collision, encounter roll, warp detection, NPC interaction
Create EncounterSystem.ts: weighted wild selection, PokemonInstance creation, phase transition to BATTLE stub
Create stubs: BattleSystem.ts (shows dialog, A-press returns to OVERWORLD), BattleRenderer.ts (minimal dialog box)
Create OverworldRenderer.ts: tile-by-tile draw with fallback, player sprite, NPC sprites, camera offset, imageSmoothingEnabled=false
Create React components: GameCanvas.tsx (canvas ref, useEffect mounts engine, 3× scale), MobileControls.tsx (touch d-pad)
Create .gitkeep files for sprite directories
Write Vitest unit tests for Player, PokemonInstance, EncounterSystem, Camera, InputManager, SaveSystem
Write Playwright E2E test: canvas renders at 480×432, arrow key input moves player
Run npm install, npm test — fix any failures
Commit all files with descriptive message
Write .af/tasks/201/pr-body.md and push

cases: Player initializes with correct default values
enqueueMove adds direction to queue
pixelOffset decrements toward 0 each tick
tileX/tileY updates when move completes
facing updates on direction change
walkFrame cycles 0→1→2→0 during movement
createPokemonInstance returns correct species ID
HP is calculated with Gen 1 formula at level 5
HP is calculated at level 50 for Pikachu
Stats (Attack/Defense/Speed/Special) use Gen 1 formula
IVs are in range 0–15
Starting moves match learnset for level
UUID is unique across two instances
selectWildPokemon returns species from encounter table
Selected level is within min/max range
Weighted selection favors higher-weight entries
PokemonInstance is created for selected species
Camera screenX/screenY is 0,0 when player at viewport center
Camera clamps to left/top edge of map
Camera clamps to right/bottom edge of map
worldToScreen converts world coordinate correctly
isPressed returns false for unpressed key
isPressed returns true after keydown event
isPressed returns false after keyup event
wasJustPressed returns true only on first frame after keydown
heldFrames increments each update while key is held
saveGame stores slot to localStorage
loadGame retrieves slot from localStorage
loadGame returns null for empty slot
hasSave returns false before first save
hasSave returns true after save
Game canvas is visible at 480×432
Canvas has data-testid='game-canvas'
Page title contains POKÉBROWSER

risk_areas.risk: pokemon.ts and moves.ts will exceed 150-line guideline — they are pure data tables, not components, so this is an acceptable exception. Noted in plan.md.
risk_areas.mitigation: Split species data into groups if needed; keep logic-free

risk_areas.risk: Vitest/jsdom does not implement 2D canvas rendering; getContext returns null
risk_areas.mitigation: Mock CanvasRenderingContext2D in test setup; test pure logic only (no rendering tests)

risk_areas.risk: jsdom RAF does not run; GameEngine cannot be instantiated in unit tests
risk_areas.mitigation: Unit tests import individual systems/entities directly, not GameEngine

risk_areas.risk: Press Start 2P font may fail to load in restricted network environments
risk_areas.mitigation: Font load failure is non-fatal; game renders with system fallback; preload link uses crossorigin=anonymous

risk_areas.area: SaveSystem.ts not in task file list
risk_areas.risk: GameEngine.ts references SaveSystem but the file is not in the explicit files list
risk_areas.mitigation: Create SaveSystem.ts as required infrastructure; it is implied by GameEngine spec

risk_areas.area: BattleSystem.ts not in task file list
risk_areas.risk: GameEngine dispatches to BattleSystem based on GamePhase but the file is not listed
risk_areas.mitigation: Create minimal BattleSystem stub; task says 'stub battle: shows dialog then returns to OVERWORLD'

risk_areas.risk: No actual tileset image is provided; OverworldRenderer must function without it
risk_areas.mitigation: AssetLoader.drawFallback() paints colored rects; all renders check for missing assets first

risk_areas.risk: Ghost→Psychic = 0 and Bug→Psychic = 0.5 are Gen 1 bugs, not widely documented
risk_areas.mitigation: Hardcode these quirk cells explicitly in the 18×18 matrix with inline comments

persistence_strategy: localStorage via SaveSystem.ts — save slots serialized as JSON under key 'pokebrowser_save_{slot}'. Map data is static JSON bundled with the app (no runtime DB). No server-side storage needed for this task.
error_handling_approach: Three-tier approach: (1) AssetLoader wraps each Image.onload in try/catch and calls drawFallback on failure; (2) GameEngine wraps update() and render() in try/catch, logs errors, and displays error overlay on canvas if loop crashes; (3) React GameCanvas component catches engine initialization errors with an error state that shows a styled message.
